what: scripted multi-turn Claude-Code-shaped loop over fak's in-kernel tool-calling path
path_under_test: agent.LiftTextToolCalls text <tool_call> lift used by the in-kernel planner
gateway adjudicateProposed for each proposed call
why_scripted: The Windows CPU host can prove the round-trip wiring quickly, but a live full Claude Code -Kernel prompt is GPU/Metal territory because CPU prefill of the full tool-schema prompt has hit the 900 s ceiling on Qwen2.5-1.5B-Q8.

turns.assistant_text: Both files are present; main.go is the entrypoint.

claim: The in-kernel Anthropic path closes the multi-turn tool_result round trip: call -> tool_result -> second call -> tool_result -> final answer, with every proposed call adjudicated.

honest_model_floor.not_floor: Qwen2.5 1.5B and similar <=3B cached models intermittently emit malformed/raw tool calls under Claude Code's large multi-tool prompt.
observed_failure_modes: truncation or timeout before a complete tool call on the CPU full-prompt path
fail-closed ToolCallsDropped when an unclosed <tool_call> is lifted
honest_model_floor.live_competence_gate: A live -Kernel --probe multi-turn run with the full Claude Code prompt still needs a GPU/Metal decode lane or much longer CPU timeouts; this artifact does not claim that live pass.
